project.name: taugo
project.description: TauGo is an executable Go kernel for the τ operator (chap. III.8 of the InteroperabiliteAgentique monograph): given an Exchange, its single public API k.Decide(ctx, x) returns a Decision selecting an output régime among Deterministe, Probabiliste, and Refus, under five refutable invariants (I1-I5) and three dimensions (D-SENS, D-AUTORITÉ, D-INVARIANT). It is a pure-local, stdlib-only library built on a strict 4-layer Clean Architecture (Apache-2.0), not a framework, orchestrator, or LLM wrapper. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.languageNotes: ISP-narrow interface (2 methods); Interpret must be deterministic at temperature 0 and powers the D-SENS reasoner-intent probe.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation for the llm bridge describing the abstract Client boundary that confines LLM non-determinism away from internal/tau.

nodes.name: stub.go
nodes.summary: Deterministic Stub implementation of the LLM Client used by default in tests, returning a fixed fingerprint and a bounded [0,1] interpretation score derived from intent length.
nodes.languageNotes: Default stub backend per project doctrine; deterministic so no external LLM call occurs without TAUGO_LLM_BACKEND=real.

nodes.name: Stub
nodes.summary: Empty struct implementing the llm.Client interface with deterministic Fingerprint and Interpret methods.

nodes.name: Interpret
nodes.summary: Returns a deterministic, bounded [0,1] interpretation score computed from the intent string length, with no external call.

nodes.name: stub_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests asserting the Stub's stable Fingerprint and that Interpret is deterministic and bounded within [0,1] across sample intents.

nodes.name: calibrate.go
nodes.summary: Core calibration logic: loads and validates a labeled corpus, runs a deterministic grid-search to pick best thresholds, and serializes profiles byte-canonically (sorted keys) for reproducible storage.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements canonical JSON marshalling via re-decode with json.Number and recursive key sorting to guarantee byte-identical profiles; CorpusEntry migrates legacy ExpectedRegime to LabeledRegime.

nodes.name: CorpusEntry
nodes.summary: Labeled calibration corpus record carrying the three dimension scores, human-in-loop and attestation flags, and the labeled regime, with migrate and Validate methods.

nodes.name: Validate
nodes.summary: Validates a CorpusEntry's score ranges and labeled regime, returning a wrapped error for any out-of-range or unknown value.

nodes.name: LoadCorpus
nodes.summary: Streams a JSONL corpus file, decoding each line into a CorpusEntry, migrating legacy fields and validating before appending.

nodes.name: Calibrate
nodes.summary: Performs a deterministic grid-search over candidate thresholds, scoring agreement against the labeled corpus and returning the profile with the best thresholds while preserving V1 weights.

nodes.name: MarshalCanonical
nodes.summary: Serializes a Profile to canonical JSON with recursively sorted keys, indentation, and a trailing newline to guarantee byte-identical output across runs.

nodes.name: UnmarshalCanonical
nodes.summary: Deserializes canonical JSON bytes back into a Profile, wrapping decode errors.

nodes.name: sortedAny
nodes.summary: Recursively rewrites decoded JSON maps and slices into key-sorted kv structures so canonical marshalling is order-stable.

nodes.name: MarshalJSON
nodes.summary: Custom JSON marshaller for the kv ordered-pair type, emitting a single-key object with preserved insertion order.

nodes.name: calibrate_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the calibration pipeline: grid-search best-thresholds selection, determinism, agreement non-regression, V1 weight preservation, canonical marshalling (sorted keys, round-trip, byte-identical, valid JSON), and corpus regime validation/migration.

nodes.name: TestMarshalCanonical_ByteIdentical
nodes.summary: Asserts that marshalling the same profile twice yields byte-identical output by comparing SHA-256 digests.

nodes.name: TestCorpus_ExpectedRegime_MigreVersLabeledRegime
nodes.summary: Verifies that a legacy corpus entry using ExpectedRegime is migrated into LabeledRegime on load (v0.1.1 retro-compat).

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation for the calibration package describing profile generation, drift detection, and reproducible serialization responsibilities.

nodes.name: drift.go
nodes.summary: Drift detection that flags a profile as stale when CPU, LLM, or corpus fingerprints mismatch the environment or the date_revision has passed, plus helpers to fingerprint the CPU and a corpus file (anti-patron #3 guard).
nodes.languageNotes: Empty fingerprints are skipped to avoid false positives; date expiry uses now.Before(DateRevision). Guards the peremption anti-pattern (invariant I3 veille).

nodes.name: DriftReport
nodes.summary: Result struct reporting whether drift was detected, which criteria triggered, and human-readable details.

nodes.name: Env
nodes.summary: Environment snapshot carrying current CPU, LLM, and corpus fingerprints against which a profile is compared for drift.

nodes.name: CheckDrift
nodes.summary: Compares a profile's pinned fingerprints and revision date against the current environment and clock, accumulating any drift criteria into a DriftReport.

nodes.name: FingerprintCorpus
nodes.summary: Computes a stable SHA-256 fingerprint of a JSONL corpus file for drift comparison, wrapping I/O errors.

nodes.name: drift_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for drift detection covering no-drift, CPU/LLM/corpus fingerprint mismatches, expired date_revision, multiple simultaneous criteria, empty-fingerprint skipping, and corpus/CPU fingerprint stability.

nodes.name: TestCheckDrift_MultipleCriteria
nodes.summary: Asserts CheckDrift reports every triggered criterion when several fingerprints mismatch and the date is expired simultaneously.

nodes.name: TestCheckDrift_DateExpired
nodes.summary: Verifies that a clock past the profile's date_revision triggers a drift criterion (Refus on peremption guard).

nodes.name: profile.go
nodes.summary: Defines the Weights and Profile data structures and DefaultProfile, the checked-in default calibration profile pinning thresholds, weights, monograph version, and a future date_revision injected by app.NewDispatcher.
nodes.languageNotes: DefaultProfile sets DateRevision at least six months ahead so the I3 peremption guard stays inert until review; imports the transverse thresholds value type (ADR-0006).

nodes.name: Profile
nodes.summary: Calibration profile struct bundling identity, version, timestamps, date_revision, pinned fingerprints, thresholds, and weights for reproducible serialization.

nodes.name: Weights
nodes.summary: Per-dimension and per-probe weight struct (D-SENS, D-AUTORITÃ‰, D-INVARIANT) consumed by the dispatcher when scoring.

nodes.name: DefaultProfile
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical default Profile with a UTC creation time, a future date_revision, pinned monograph version, default thresholds, and weights summing to one per dimension.

nodes.name: profile_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests asserting DefaultProfile's fields are non-empty, date_revision is after creation and at least six months ahead, weights sum to one per dimension, and threshold ordering invariants hold.

nodes.name: TestDefaultProfile_WeightsSumToOne
nodes.summary: Verifies each dimension's probe weights in DefaultProfile sum to one within tolerance.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Filesystem profile store that saves profiles byte-canonically, maintains a current pointer (symlink with copy fallback), loads by id/version or current, and exports a file's SHA-256.
nodes.languageNotes: refreshCurrent prefers an os.Symlink for current.json then falls back to a byte copy when symlinks are unavailable (Windows), keeping cross-platform reproducibility.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Directory-backed profile store exposing Path, Save, Load, LoadCurrent, and ExportSHA256 over a base Dir.

nodes.name: Save
nodes.summary: Marshals a profile canonically, writes it to its id/version path, and refreshes the current pointer, wrapping each failure stage.

nodes.name: refreshCurrent
nodes.summary: Points current.json at the latest profile via symlink, falling back to a file copy when symlink creation fails (Windows path).

nodes.name: ExportSHA256
nodes.summary: Streams a file through SHA-256 and returns its hex digest, wrapping open and copy errors.

nodes.name: store_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the profile Store covering save/load round-trip, byte-identical repeated saves, current pointer correctness, overwrite-on-update, corrupt/missing JSON errors, and forced mkdir/write/refresh failures.

nodes.name: TestStore_SaveTwiceSameProfile_ByteIdentical
nodes.summary: Asserts that saving the same profile twice produces byte-identical files, confirming reproducible serialization through the store.

nodes.name: store_unix_test.go
nodes.summary: Unix-tagged test verifying that Save creates a symlink for current.json on platforms supporting symlinks.
nodes.languageNotes: Build-constrained to non-Windows; checks os.Lstat mode for the symlink bit.

nodes.name: store_windows_test.go
nodes.summary: Windows-tagged test verifying that Save falls back to a real file copy of current.json when symlink creation is unavailable.
nodes.languageNotes: Build-constrained to Windows; validates the copy-fallback branch of refreshCurrent by reading current.json contents.

nodes.name: drift-corpus-a.jsonl
nodes.summary: JSONL corpus fixture A used to verify FingerprintCorpus produces distinct, stable SHA-256 digests for differing inputs.

nodes.name: drift-corpus-b.jsonl
nodes.summary: JSONL corpus fixture B paired with corpus A to confirm distinct corpora yield distinct fingerprints in drift tests.

nodes.name: mini-corpus.jsonl
nodes.summary: Thirty-entry labeled mini-corpus fixture driving calibration grid-search, determinism, and agreement tests.

nodes.name: thresholds_atomic.go
nodes.summary: Lock-free thresholds container backing each decision threshold (Deterministe, Probabiliste, AuthBlock, SensCoherence, InvCoherence, HysteresisGap) with atomic int64 cells, enforcing the Deterministe > Probabiliste ordering invariant at construction and tuning time.
nodes.languageNotes: Floats are stored as fixed-point millis in atomic.Int64 cells to allow lock-free concurrent reads (millis/fromMillis helpers); ordering violations panic as an internal-invariant breach.

nodes.name: AtomicThresholds
nodes.summary: Holds the six Ï„ decision thresholds as atomic int64 cells, exposing typed float64 getters, a Snapshot accessor, and a SetTuning mutator that re-validates the ordering invariant.

nodes.name: NewAtomicThresholds
nodes.summary: Constructs an AtomicThresholds from a plain Thresholds value, panicking if the Deterministe > Probabiliste ordering invariant is violated.

nodes.name: Snapshot
nodes.summary: Returns a consistent plain-value copy of all six thresholds for read-only consumers such as the dispatcher.

nodes.name: SetTuning
nodes.summary: Atomically replaces all threshold cells with a new tuning, re-validating the Deterministe > Probabiliste ordering invariant before storing.

nodes.name: thresholds_atomic_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies AtomicThresholds roundtrip fidelity, the ordering invariant (panic on Deterministe â‰¤ Probabiliste), SetTuning behaviour, and race-free concurrent reads.

nodes.name: weights.go
nodes.summary: Defines CalibrateWeights, the V1 dimension-weight calibration entry point, which is a deterministic pass-through over the base profile weights via a pluggable WeightHook strategy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a function-typed WeightHook seam to keep the V1 pass-through swappable for future calibration strategies without changing the call site.

nodes.name: weights_test.go
nodes.summary: Confirms CalibrateWeights is a V1 pass-through, preserves the sum-to-one weight constraint, allows a custom WeightHook strategy, and reports the current strategy as V1.

nodes.name: errors.go
nodes.summary: Typed error layer (ADR-0009) defining DispatchError, RefusError, and CalibrationError plus the errors.Is/As-compatible sentinels used across the Ï„ kernel for stage-attributed failures and Refus diagnostics.
nodes.languageNotes: Each error carries the dispatch stage and wraps a cause via Unwrap to support fmt.Errorf("%w") chaining and errors.Is matching against package sentinels.

nodes.name: DispatchError
nodes.summary: Stage-attributed dispatch failure carrying the exchange ID, a detail string, and a wrapped cause exposed via Unwrap.

nodes.name: RefusError
nodes.summary: Represents a Refus decision as an error value, carrying the dispatch stage, canonical diagnostic, and exchange ID.

nodes.name: CalibrationError
nodes.summary: Calibration-profile failure carrying the offending profile version and a wrapped cause exposed via Unwrap.

nodes.name: errors_test.go
nodes.summary: Exercises Error() message formatting and Unwrap propagation for the three typed errors and confirms the package sentinels are identifiable via errors.Is.

nodes.name: decision_invariants_test.go
nodes.summary: Guards the cross-cutting Decision invariants: every Decision is traced, a Refus always carries a diagnostic, and the returned Trace is immutable against caller mutation.

nodes.name: dispatcher.go
nodes.summary: Core orchestration engine implementing Kernel.Decide as the 8-step pseudo-algorithm: frontier check, D-AUTORITÃ‰ ontological lock, profile expiry guard, ventilated D-SENS/D-INVARIANT scoring, weighted regime classification, and I1-I5 invariant evaluation, always returning a fully traced Decision.
nodes.languageNotes: Injectable clock (WithClock) keeps the expiry guard deterministic under test; refusDecision/refusDecisionWithScores helpers build fully traced Refus outcomes at each early-exit step.

nodes.name: Dispatcher
nodes.summary: Holds the injected LLM client, atomic thresholds, calibration profile, and clock, exposing Decide as the kernel's single decision entry point.

nodes.name: NewDispatcher
nodes.summary: Constructs a Dispatcher from an LLM client and ordered thresholds, panicking if the thresholds violate their ordering invariant.

nodes.name: Decide
nodes.summary: Runs the 8-step Ï„ decision pipeline over an Exchange â€” frontier, D-AUTORITÃ‰ lock, profile expiry, ventilated dimension scoring, weighted regime classification, and I1-I5 evaluation â€” returning a Regime or Refus with a complete ventilated Trace.

nodes.name: dimensionWeights
nodes.summary: Resolves the D-SENS/D-AUTORITÃ‰/D-INVARIANT aggregation weights from the active calibration profile, falling back to defaults when no profile is set.

nodes.name: refusDecisionWithScores
nodes.summary: Builds a Refus Decision carrying a diagnostic, frontier result, and the ventilated D-AUTORITÃ‰/D-SENS/D-INVARIANT scores in its Trace.

nodes.name: dispatcher_expiry_test.go
nodes.summary: Covers step 3 of Decide: an expired profile (today > date_revision) yields Refus, a fresh profile proceeds, and a zero DateRevision or nil profile skips the expiry guard.

nodes.name: dispatcher_invariants_test.go
nodes.summary: Covers step 8 of Decide: I1-I5 invariant evaluation leaves the Trace empty when no violation occurs, enriches it on violation, and never mutates the classified Regime.

nodes.name: dispatcher_profile_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies the Decision echoes the profile version and DateRevision and that the dispatcher applies profile-supplied dimension weights, contrasting default-weight versus profile-weight outcomes.

nodes.name: dispatcher_scores_test.go
nodes.summary: Confirms the Trace carries the ventilated D-SENS/D-AUTORITÃ‰/D-INVARIANT scores and that EvaluateI3/EvaluateI4 read those ventilated scores (ADR-0008) rather than the proxy TauScore.

nodes.name: dispatcher_test.go
nodes.summary: Primary dispatcher test suite with shared Exchange builders and a fakeLLM stub, covering the Deterministe, Probabiliste, and hysteresis-defaults-to-Deterministe regime outcomes of Decide.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation stub for the orchestration package housing the dispatcher and Decision types.

nodes.name: guards_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the first-rank refusal guards: D-AUTORITÃ‰ ontological lock (Refus without AttestationInstitutionnelle, pass with attestation) and I4 coherence (incoherent s<Î¸_sens âˆ§ iâ‰¥Î¸_inv refused, coherent combination accepted).

nodes.name: thresholds.go
nodes.summary: Provides DefaultThresholds, the canonical default decision-threshold set, aliasing the transverse internal/thresholds value type (ADR-0006) for the orchestration layer.

nodes.name: DefaultThresholds
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical default decision thresholds (Deterministe, Probabiliste, AuthBlock, coherence, hysteresis) used to construct a Dispatcher.

nodes.name: diagnostics.go
nodes.summary: Declares the canonical Refus diagnostic constants (DiagFrontiereFranchie, DiagVerrouOntologique, DiagPeremptionProfile, DiagIncoherenceI4) referenced across the kernel.

nodes.name: dauthority.go
nodes.summary: Computes the D-AUTORITÃ‰ dimension score [0,1] â€” the reach of the delegation chain â€” by weighting four probes (chain depth, cross-org, human anchor, dynamic resolution) and reporting a ventilated Score with its probe breakdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Probe weights are supplied via an AuthorityWeights struct that must sum to one; clamp01 bounds the aggregate to [0,1].

nodes.name: ScoreDAuthority
nodes.summary: Aggregates the four D-AUTORITÃ‰ probes under the supplied weights into a clamped [0,1] Score, populating the per-probe breakdown for the ventilated Trace.

nodes.name: DefaultAuthorityWeights
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical AuthorityWeights (chain depth, cross-org, human anchor, dynamic resolution) summing to one.

nodes.name: dauthority_test.go
nodes.summary: Validates D-AUTORITÃ‰ scoring: boundedness in [0,1], short chains scoring below long chains, probe weights summing to one, populated probe map, chain-depth saturation, and empty-org handling.

nodes.name: dinvariant.go
nodes.summary: Computes the D-INVARIANT dimension score [0,1] â€” support for integration invariants â€” by weighting four probes (event registry, idempotency, capability mediation, enumerated plan) into a ventilated Score with its probe breakdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Probe weights are supplied via an InvariantWeights struct that must sum to one; clamp01 bounds the aggregate to [0,1].

nodes.name: ScoreDInvariant
nodes.summary: Aggregates the four D-INVARIANT probes under the supplied weights into a clamped [0,1] Score, populating the per-probe breakdown for the ventilated Trace.

nodes.name: DefaultInvariantWeights
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical InvariantWeights (event registry, idempotency, capability mediation, enumerated plan) summing to one.

nodes.name: dinvariant_test.go
nodes.summary: Validates D-INVARIANT scoring: boundedness in [0,1], frozen support scoring below traced support, probe weights summing to one, populated probe map, explicit enumerated-plan and dynamic-discovery capability-mediation cases.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation for the dimensions package implementing the three orthogonal Ï„ dimension scorers D-SENS, D-AUTORITÃ‰, and D-INVARIANT.

nodes.name: dsens.go
nodes.summary: Computes the D-SENS dimension score [0,1] â€” the locus where meaning is fixed â€” by weighting four probes (contract, runtime resolve, capability discovery, reasoner intent), the last consulting the injected LLM client through its narrow interface.
nodes.languageNotes: Only the reasoner-intent probe touches the LLM client, isolated behind the bridge/llm interface so non-determinism never leaks into the rest of tau/*; clamp01 bounds the aggregate to [0,1].

nodes.name: ScoreDSens
nodes.summary: Aggregates the four D-SENS probes under the supplied weights into a clamped [0,1] Score, invoking the LLM client only for the reasoner-intent probe and populating the ventilated Trace breakdown.

nodes.name: DefaultSensWeights
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical SensWeights (contract, runtime resolve, capability discovery, reasoner intent) summing to one.

nodes.name: dsens_test.go
nodes.summary: Validates D-SENS scoring: boundedness in [0,1], static exchanges scoring below dynamic ones, probe weights summing to one, populated probe map, empty-intent handling, and graceful behaviour when the LLM client returns an error.

nodes.name: score.go
nodes.summary: Aliases tau.Score into the dimensions package to break the import cycle ADR-0008 would otherwise create, and provides the shared clamp01 [0,1] bounding helper used by all three dimension scorers.
nodes.languageNotes: Score is a type alias (type Score = tau.Score), so struct-literal construction in this package keeps working while Trace can carry the type without an import cycle.

nodes.name: score_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the clamp01 helper in the dimensions package, covering below-zero, above-one, boundary and NaN inputs to guarantee D-SENS/D-AUTORITE/D-INVARIANT scores stay in [0,1].

nodes.name: doc.go

tour.title: Project Overview & Doctrine
tour.description: Start here to learn what TauGo is — and, just as importantly, what it refuses to be. README.md states the doctrine and anti-objectifs: TauGo is the executable kernel of the opérateur τ, exposing a single public API that returns a régime (Deterministe | Probabiliste | Refus), never a peer's behavior. PRD.md is the canonical specification covering the frontière de validité, the three dimensions, and the five refutable invariants the rest of the tour explores.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: cmd/tau/main.go is the binary's outermost layer: it parses the decide and calibrate subcommands and runs a JSON-in/JSON-out flow by calling app.NewDispatcher. This is the concrete moment where a real Exchange enters the kernel, so it is the natural second stop after understanding the project's purpose. Notice it does almost no logic itself — it delegates immediately inward, which is the Clean Architecture discipline you will see enforced later.

tour.title: Application Wiring
tour.description: Building on the CLI from Step 2, internal/app/app.go is where dependency injection happens: NewDispatcher wires the checked-in DefaultProfile and the selected LLM backend (the deterministic stub by default) into an orchestration dispatcher. This single function activates the péremption guard by always loading a profile, so no caller can accidentally run with an unbounded model. agentmesh.go shows the alternate ingress — bridging AgentMeshKafka exchanges into a streamed channel of tau.Exchange values.

tour.title: The 8-Step Dispatcher (Decide)
tour.description: internal/orchestration/dispatcher.go is the heart of the runtime: it implements Kernel.Decide as a non-arbitrary 8-step pipeline — frontière check, D-AUTORITÉ ontological lock, profile-expiry guard, ventilated D-SENS/D-INVARIANT scoring, weighted régime classification, then I1-I5 invariant evaluation — and always returns a fully traced Decision. With the highest fan-out in the whole graph (33), this file is the spine that connects every layer you will visit next. thresholds.go supplies the canonical DefaultThresholds it reads at the classification step.

tour.title: Domain Core — opérateur τ & the frontière Guard
tour.description: operator.go is the sole public entry point of the τ core (the most depended-upon file in the project, fan-in 44): it declares the Kernel.Decide interface and the canonical types Exchange, Decision, Trace, Score, Regime, and DiscoveryMode. frontier.go is the first-rank guard — FrontierCheck.Inside() returns true only when none of the four classic frontière conditions are met, gating whether the kernel may proceed; bypassing it is forbidden (anti-patron #2). diagnostics.go names the canonical Refus reasons referenced everywhere else.
tour.languageLesson: Go keeps a package's public surface narrow: only capitalized identifiers (Kernel, Decide, Exchange) are exported. Concentrating the entire API in operator.go means the compiler itself enforces that the rest of internal/tau stays private — a structural guard, not a convention.

tour.title: The Three Dimensions
tour.description: Each call is scored along three orthogonal dimensions in [0,1], computed by dsens.go (D-SENS — where meaning is fixed), dauthority.go (D-AUTORITÉ — the reach of the delegation chain), and dinvariant.go (D-INVARIANT — support for integration invariants). Every scorer weights four probes and returns a ventilated Score with its probe breakdown, which is exactly what the dispatcher records in the Trace from Step 4. Only D-SENS consults the injected LLM client, and even then through the narrow interface you meet in Step 9.
tour.languageLesson: score.go uses a Go type alias (type Score = tau.Score) to share the type across packages without creating an import cycle that would otherwise violate the layering ADR-0008 — a lightweight idiom for re-exposing a type a leaf package must not own.

tour.title: The Five Invariants I1-I5 (+ Fuzz)
tour.description: evaluator.go aggregates the five refutable invariants into one Statuses struct, run by EvaluateInvariants at the final dispatcher step. Each invariant encodes a theoretical claim from chap. III.8.5: I1 (τ conserves the magnitude — moves t_fix, not content), I3 (D-AUTORITÉ asymmetry per Searle 1995, forcing an ontological Refus when authority is blocked without attestation or the profile is périmé), I4 (the silent-rupture coherence constraint), and I5 (composed piles inherit the conjunction). fuzz_targets_test.go refutes property violations under randomized inputs.

tour.title: Native Go Fuzzing of Invariants
tour.description: The invariants are not asserted only on hand-picked cases — fuzz_targets_test.go declares FuzzI1..FuzzI5 with makeFuzzExchange/fuzzRefTime helpers that hammer each evaluator with randomized Exchanges to actively try to falsify its property (chap. III.8.7). This is the empirical backbone behind the 'refutable' status of every invariant in Step 7, and it runs at millions of executions per second.
tour.languageLesson: Go's built-in fuzzing: a func FuzzXxx(f *testing.F) seeds inputs with f.Add(...) and explores mutations via f.Fuzz(func(t, args...){...}). Seed corpora live under testdata/fuzz/, and any failing input is automatically saved there as a regression — property-based testing with zero external dependencies.

tour.title: Calibration, Drift & Péremption
tour.description: The régime thresholds the dispatcher uses are not magic numbers — they come from a Profile. profile.go defines DefaultProfile, the checked-in profile pinning thresholds, weights, the monograph version, and a date_revision. calibrate.go runs a deterministic grid-search over a labeled corpus and serializes profiles byte-canonically (sorted keys) for reproducibility. drift.go flags a profile as stale when CPU/LLM/corpus fingerprints mismatch or date_revision has passed — the guard behind anti-patron #3 and the I3 péremption Refus from Step 7.

tour.title: The Bridges — Isolating Non-Determinism
tour.description: TauGo never embeds a concrete LLM. client.go defines the narrow Client interface (Fingerprint + Interpret) that the τ core consumes, and stub.go is the deterministic default used in every test — a fixed fingerprint and a bounded [0,1] score derived from intent length. On the empirical side, file_adapter.go streams JSONL fixtures as neutral DTOs (standing in for real Kafka), and classifier.go scores those decisions against the I4 constraint to produce the campaign report. Together these bridges keep non-determinism out of the core you saw in Steps 5-7.
tour.languageLesson: This is Go's interface-at-the-consumer idiom (ISP): the τ core declares the small Client interface it needs, and concrete implementations (the stub, or a future real backend) satisfy it implicitly — no 'implements' keyword. Dependency inversion falls out for free, which is what lets every test run fully deterministically.

tour.title: Étanchéité & Anti-Pattern Guards
tour.description: The Clean Architecture you have been tracing is not just a diagram — it is enforced by tests. arch_test.go walks the AST to forbid the bridge from importing tau/*, forbids any concrete LLM import inside the domain (anti-patron #6), and pins the layering rules. anti_patterns_test.go guards the rest: no exported predictive API (Predict*/Forecast*), the I3 date_revision is respected, and unmodeled observations are always reported. A red test here means an architectural boundary was eroded.
tour.languageLesson: Go's go/ast and go/parser packages let tests inspect source as data: these guards parse every file in a package and walk the syntax tree to assert that forbidden import paths or method-name patterns never appear — compile-time architecture rules expressed as ordinary unit tests.

tour.title: Putting It Together — Quickstart & Shared Kernel
tour.description: examples/quickstart/main.go closes the loop: it builds a dispatcher via app.NewDispatcher (Step 3) and calls Decide on three typed cases, printing each Regime, the ventilated D-SENS/D-AUTORITÉ/D-INVARIANT scores (Step 6), and the JSON Trace — a runnable summary of the entire pipeline. Underneath, the shared kernel keeps the layers honest: thresholds.go is the transverse value type that depends on no other taugo package (ADR-0006), and errors.go provides the typed DispatchError/RefusError/CalibrationError sentinels (ADR-0009) carried through every Refus.
